summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Residual pattern'?

"Residual pattern" means: A curved or systematic pattern in the residual plot indicates the linear model is inadequate and a transformation or different model is needed. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Log transformation'?

"Log transformation" means: Taking log(y) to linearize exponential growth; taking log(x) to linearize logarithmic relationships; taking both for power models. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Exponential regression'?

"Exponential regression" means: Fits y = ab^x by regressing log(y) against x; the slope of the transformed line gives log(b). The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Power regression'?

"Power regression" means: Fits y = ax^b by regressing log(y) against log(x); the slope of the transformed line gives b. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Model selection'?

"Model selection" means: Compare R² values, residual plots, and prediction accuracy across candidate models to choose the best-fitting transformation. The other options describe different concepts.
